title: Semele
content:
Semele

Matter not if his fate was too harsh. Diana decided. And she derided thrice. Dripping poison into the ears of her charmed.
Then I was spread into every corner, growing and boiling, and bubbling: waiting to be lapped up by the eager curious tongues fed by the ears and let loose to rage by the mouth: waiting to be typed texted and hexed from screen to screen. Bounced from eye to prying eye, reincarnated reimagined.
They never told me like I was. Each time they told me: I broke. Shattered. A fractured faulted vase forced together again and again.
Private not public, until enough so many private becomes public.
Shot across to every mobile, decoded upon by every screen by another staring set of absorbing eyes.
Eyes. Her eyes.
I care not for such trappings nor snares, unless it is those trappings or snares that somewayhow trap or snare we.
I do care on Semele. Just as he cares in Semele.
Cared enough, plugged her with life.
Pregnant and glowing.
Left and growling.
He goes. Always at night.
Why do you come at night?
